endpoints.name: Get all Actions
endpoints.description: Returns all Action objects. You can `sort` the results by using the sort URI parameter, and filter them with the `status` parameter.
endpoints.notes: No authentication required.

endpoints.name: Get an Action
endpoints.description: Returns a specific Action object.
endpoints.notes: No authentication required. Replace {{ID}} with a real id.

endpoints.name: Get all Certificates
endpoints.description: Returns all Certificate objects.
endpoints.notes: No authentication required.

endpoints.name: Create a Certificate
endpoints.description:
Creates a new Certificate.

The default type **uploaded** allows for uploading your existing `certificate` and `private_key` in PEM format. You have to monitor its expiration date and handle renewal yourself.

In contrast, type **managed** requests a new Certificate from *Let's Encrypt* for the specified `domain_names`. Only domains managed by *Hetzner DNS* are supported. We handle renewal and timely alert the project owner via email if problems occur.

For type `managed` Certificates the `action` key of the response contains the Action that allows for tracking the issuance process. For type `uploaded` Certificates the `action` is always null.
endpoints.notes: No authentication required.

endpoints.name: Get a Certificate
endpoints.description: Gets a specific Certificate object.
endpoints.notes: No authentication required. Replace {{ID}} with a real id.

endpoints.name: Update a Certificate
endpoints.description:
Updates the Certificate properties.

Note that when updating labels, the Certificate’s current set of labels will be replaced with the labels provided in the request body. So, for example, if you want to add a new label, you have to provide all existing labels plus the new label in the request body.

Note: if the Certificate object changes during the request, the response will be a “conflict” error.
endpoints.notes: No authentication required. Replace {{ID}} with a real id.

endpoints.name: Delete a Certificate
endpoints.description: Deletes a Certificate.
endpoints.notes: No authentication required. Replace {{ID}} with a real id.

endpoints.name: Get all Actions for a Certificate
endpoints.description:
Returns all Action objects for a Certificate. You can sort the results by using the `sort` URI parameter, and filter them with the `status` parameter.

Only type `managed` Certificates can have Actions. For type `uploaded` Certificates the `actions` key will always contain an empty array.
endpoints.notes: No authentication required. Replace {{ID}} with a real id.

endpoints.name: Retry Issuance or Renewal
endpoints.description:
Retry a failed Certificate issuance or renewal.

Only applicable if the type of the Certificate is `managed` and the issuance or renewal status is `failed`.

#### Call specific error codes

| Code                                                    | Description                                                               |
|---------------------------------------------------------|---------------------------------------------------------------------------|
| `caa_record_does_not_allow_ca`                          | CAA record does not allow certificate authority                           |
| `ca_dns_validation_failed`                              | Certificate Authority: DNS validation failed                              |
| `ca_too_many_authorizations_failed_recently`            | Certificate Authority: Too many authorizations failed recently            |
| `ca_too_many_certificates_issued_for_registered_domain` | Certificate Authority: Too many certificates issued for registered domain |
| `ca_too_many_duplicate_certificates`                    | Certificate Authority: Too many duplicate certificates                    |
| `could_not_verify_domain_delegated_to_zone`             | Could not verify domain delegated to zone                                 |
| `dns_zone_not_found`                                    | DNS zone not found                                                        |
| `dns_zone_is_secondary_zone`                            | DNS zone is a secondary zone                                              |
endpoints.notes: No authentication required. Replace {{ID}} with a real id.

endpoints.name: Get an Action for a Certificate
endpoints.description: Returns a specific Action for a Certificate. Only type `managed` Certificates have Actions.
endpoints.notes: No authentication required. Replace {{ID}} with a real id. Replace {{ACTION_ID}} with a real action_id.

endpoints.name: Get all Datacenters
endpoints.description: Returns all Datacenter objects.
endpoints.notes: No authentication required.

endpoints.name: Get a Datacenter
endpoints.description: Returns a specific Datacenter object.
endpoints.notes: No authentication required. Replace {{ID}} with a real id.

endpoints.name: Get all Firewalls
endpoints.description: Returns all Firewall objects.
endpoints.notes: No authentication required.

endpoints.name: Create a Firewall
endpoints.description:
Creates a new Firewall.

#### Call specific error codes

| Code                          | Description                                                   |
|------------------------------ |-------------------------------------------------------------- |
| `server_already_added`        | Server added more than one time to resource                   |
| `incompatible_network_type`   | The Network type is incompatible for the given resource       |
| `firewall_resource_not_found` | The resource the Firewall should be attached to was not found |
endpoints.notes: No authentication required.

endpoints.name: Get a Firewall
endpoints.description: Gets a specific Firewall object.
endpoints.notes: No authentication required. Replace {{ID}} with a real id.

endpoints.name: Update a Firewall
endpoints.description:
Updates the Firewall.

Note that when updating labels, the Firewall's current set of labels will be replaced with the labels provided in the request body. So, for example, if you want to add a new label, you have to provide all existing labels plus the new label in the request body.

Note: if the Firewall object changes during the request, the response will be a “conflict” error.
endpoints.notes: No authentication required. Replace {{ID}} with a real id.

endpoints.name: Delete a Firewall
endpoints.description:
Deletes a Firewall.

#### Call specific error codes

| Code                 | Description                               |
|--------------------- |-------------------------------------------|
| `resource_in_use`    | Firewall must not be in use to be deleted |
endpoints.notes: No authentication required. Replace {{ID}} with a real id.

endpoints.name: Get all Actions for a Firewall
endpoints.description: Returns all Action objects for a Firewall. You can sort the results by using the `sort` URI parameter, and filter them with the `status` parameter.
endpoints.notes: No authentication required. Replace {{ID}} with a real id.

endpoints.name: Apply to Resources
endpoints.description:
Applies one Firewall to multiple resources.

Currently servers (public network interface) and label selectors are supported.

#### Call specific error codes

| Code                          | Description                                                   |
|-------------------------------|---------------------------------------------------------------|
| `firewall_already_applied`    | Firewall was already applied on resource                      |
| `incompatible_network_type`   | The Network type is incompatible for the given resource       |
| `firewall_resource_not_found` | The resource the Firewall should be attached to was not found |
endpoints.notes: No authentication required. Replace {{ID}} with a real id.

endpoints.name: Remove from Resources
endpoints.description:
Removes one Firewall from multiple resources.

Currently only Servers (and their public network interfaces) are supported.

#### Call specific error codes

| Code                                  | Description                                                            |
|---------------------------------------|------------------------------------------------------------------------|
| `firewall_already_removed`            | Firewall was already removed from the resource                         |
| `firewall_resource_not_found`         | The resource the Firewall should be attached to was not found          |
| `firewall_managed_by_label_selector`  | Firewall was applied via label selector and cannot be removed manually |
endpoints.notes: No authentication required. Replace {{ID}} with a real id.

endpoints.name: Set Rules
endpoints.description:
Sets the rules of a Firewall.

All existing rules will be overwritten. Pass an empty `rules` array to remove all rules.
The maximum amount of rules that can be defined is 50.

#### Call specific error codes

| Code                          | Description                                                   |
|-------------------------------|---------------------------------------------------------------|
| `firewall_resource_not_found` | The resource the Firewall should be attached to was not found |
endpoints.notes: No authentication required. Replace {{ID}} with a real id.

endpoints.name: Get an Action for a Firewall
endpoints.description: Returns a specific Action for a Firewall.
endpoints.notes: No authentication required. Replace {{ID}} with a real id. Replace {{ACTION_ID}} with a real action_id.

endpoints.name: Get all Floating IPs
endpoints.description: Returns all Floating IP objects.
endpoints.notes: No authentication required.

endpoints.name: Create a Floating IP
endpoints.description: Creates a new Floating IP assigned to a Server. If you want to create a Floating IP that is not bound to a Server, you need to provide the `home_location` key instead of `server`. This can be either the ID or the name of the Location this IP shall be created in. Note that a Floating IP can be assigned to a Server in any Location later on. For optimal routing it is advised to use the Floating IP in the same Location it was created in.
endpoints.notes: No authentication required.

endpoints.name: Get a Floating IP
endpoints.description: Returns a specific Floating IP object.
endpoints.notes: No authentication required. Replace {{ID}} with a real id.

endpoints.name: Update a Floating IP
endpoints.description:
Updates the description or labels of a Floating IP.
Also note that when updating labels, the Floating IP’s current set of labels will be replaced with the labels provided in the request body. So, for example, if you want to add a new label, you have to provide all existing labels plus the new label in the request body.
endpoints.notes: No authentication required. Replace {{ID}} with a real id.

endpoints.name: Delete a Floating IP
endpoints.description: Deletes a Floating IP. If it is currently assigned to a Server it will automatically get unassigned.
endpoints.notes: No authentication required. Replace {{ID}} with a real id.

endpoints.name: Get all Actions for a Floating IP
endpoints.description: Returns all Action objects for a Floating IP. You can sort the results by using the `sort` URI parameter, and filter them with the `status` parameter.
endpoints.notes: No authentication required. Replace {{ID}} with a real id.

endpoints.name: Assign a Floating IP to a Server
endpoints.description: Assigns a Floating IP to a Server.
endpoints.notes: No authentication required. Replace {{ID}} with a real id.

endpoints.name: Change reverse DNS entry for a Floating IP
endpoints.description: Changes the hostname that will appear when getting the hostname belonging to this Floating IP.
endpoints.notes: No authentication required. Replace {{ID}} with a real id.

endpoints.name: Change Floating IP Protection
endpoints.description: Changes the protection configuration of the Floating IP.
endpoints.notes: No authentication required. Replace {{ID}} with a real id.

endpoints.name: Unassign a Floating IP
endpoints.description: Unassigns a Floating IP, resulting in it being unreachable. You may assign it to a Server again at a later time.
endpoints.notes: No authentication required. Replace {{ID}} with a real id.

endpoints.name: Get an Action for a Floating IP

endpoints.notes: No authentication required. Replace {{ID}} with a real id. Replace {{ACTION_ID}} with a real action_id.

endpoints.name: Change Image Protection

endpoints.name: Get all ISOs

endpoints.name: Get an ISO

endpoints.notes: No authentication required. Replace {{ID}} with a real id.

endpoints.name: Get all Load Balancer Types
endpoints.description: Gets all Load Balancer type objects.

endpoints.name: Get a Load Balancer Type
endpoints.description: Gets a specific Load Balancer type object.
endpoints.notes: No authentication required. Replace {{ID}} with a real id.

endpoints.name: Get all Load Balancers
endpoints.description: Gets all existing Load Balancers that you have available.
endpoints.notes: No authentication required.

endpoints.name: Create a Load Balancer
endpoints.description:
Creates a Load Balancer.

#### Call specific error codes

| Code                                    | Description                                                                                           |
|-----------------------------------------|-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
| `cloud_resource_ip_not_allowed`         | The IP you are trying to add as a target belongs to a Hetzner Cloud resource                          |
| `ip_not_owned`                          | The IP is not owned by the owner of the project of the Load Balancer                                  |
| `load_balancer_not_attached_to_network` | The Load Balancer is not attached to a network                                                        |
| `robot_unavailable`                     | Robot was not available. The caller may retry the operation after a short delay.                      |
| `server_not_attached_to_network`        | The server you are trying to add as a target is not attached to the same network as the Load Balancer |
| `source_port_already_used`              | The source port you are trying to add is already in use                                               |
| `target_already_defined`                | The Load Balancer target you are trying to define is already defined                                  |
endpoints.notes: No authentication required.

endpoints.name: Get a Load Balancer
endpoints.description: Gets a specific Load Balancer object.
endpoints.notes: No authentication required. Replace {{ID}} with a real id.

endpoints.name: Update a Load Balancer
endpoints.description:
Updates a Load Balancer. You can update a Load Balancer’s name and a Load Balancer’s labels.

Note that when updating labels, the Load Balancer’s current set of labels will be replaced with the labels provided in the request body. So, for example, if you want to add a new label, you have to provide all existing labels plus the new label in the request body.

Note: if the Load Balancer object changes during the request, the response will be a “conflict” error.
endpoints.notes: No authentication required. Replace {{ID}} with a real id.

endpoints.name: Delete a Load Balancer
endpoints.description: Deletes a Load Balancer.
endpoints.notes: No authentication required. Replace {{ID}} with a real id.

endpoints.name: Get all Actions for a Load Balancer
endpoints.description: Returns all Action objects for a Load Balancer. You can sort the results by using the `sort` URI parameter, and filter them with the `status` parameter.
endpoints.notes: No authentication required. Replace {{ID}} with a real id.

endpoints.name: Add Service
endpoints.description:
Adds a service to a Load Balancer.

#### Call specific error codes

| Code                       | Description                                             |
|----------------------------|---------------------------------------------------------|
| `source_port_already_used` | The source port you are trying to add is already in use |
endpoints.notes: No authentication required. Replace {{ID}} with a real id.

endpoints.name: Add Target
endpoints.description:
Adds a target to a Load Balancer.

#### Call specific error codes

| Code                                    | Description                                                                                           |
|-----------------------------------------|-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
| `cloud_resource_ip_not_allowed`         | The IP you are trying to add as a target belongs to a Hetzner Cloud resource                          |
| `ip_not_owned`                          | The IP you are trying to add as a target is not owned by the Project owner                            |
| `load_balancer_not_attached_to_network` | The Load Balancer is not attached to a network                                                        |
| `robot_unavailable`                     | Robot was not available. The caller may retry the operation after a short delay.                      |
| `server_not_attached_to_network`        | The server you are trying to add as a target is not attached to the same network as the Load Balancer |
| `target_already_defined`                | The Load Balancer target you are trying to define is already defined                                  |
endpoints.notes: No authentication required. Replace {{ID}} with a real id.

endpoints.name: Attach a Load Balancer to a Network
endpoints.description:
Attach a Load Balancer to a Network.

**Call specific error codes**

| Code                             | Description                                                           |
|----------------------------------|-----------------------------------------------------------------------|
| `load_balancer_already_attached` | The Load Balancer is already attached to a network                    |
| `ip_not_available`               | The provided Network IP is not available                              |
| `no_subnet_available`            | No Subnet or IP is available for the Load Balancer within the network |
endpoints.notes: No authentication required. Replace {{ID}} with a real id.

endpoints.name: Change Algorithm
endpoints.description: Change the algorithm that determines to which target new requests are sent.
endpoints.notes: No authentication required. Replace {{ID}} with a real id.

endpoints.name: Change reverse DNS entry for this Load Balancer
endpoints.description:
Changes the hostname that will appear when getting the hostname belonging to the public IPs (IPv4 and IPv6) of this Load Balancer.

Floating IPs assigned to the Server are not affected by this.
endpoints.notes: No authentication required. Replace {{ID}} with a real id.

endpoints.name: Change Load Balancer Protection
endpoints.description: Changes the protection configuration of a Load Balancer.
endpoints.notes: No authentication required. Replace {{ID}} with a real id.

endpoints.name: Change the Type of a Load Balancer
endpoints.description:
Changes the type (Max Services, Max Targets and Max Connections) of a Load Balancer.

**Call specific error codes**

| Code                         | Description                                                     |
|------------------------------|-----------------------------------------------------------------|
| `invalid_load_balancer_type` | The Load Balancer type does not fit for the given Load Balancer |
endpoints.notes: No authentication required. Replace {{ID}} with a real id.

endpoints.name: Delete Service
endpoints.description: Delete a service of a Load Balancer.
endpoints.notes: No authentication required. Replace {{ID}} with a real id.

endpoints.name: Detach a Load Balancer from a Network
endpoints.description: Detaches a Load Balancer from a network.
endpoints.notes: No authentication required. Replace {{ID}} with a real id.

endpoints.name: Disable the public interface of a Load Balancer
endpoints.description:
Disable the public interface of a Load Balancer. The Load Balancer will be not accessible from the internet via its public IPs.

#### Call specific error codes

| Code                                      | Description                                                                    |
|-------------------------------------------|--------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
| `load_balancer_not_attached_to_network`   |  The Load Balancer is not attached to a network                                |
| `targets_without_use_private_ip`          | The Load Balancer has targets that use the public IP instead of the private IP |
endpoints.notes: No authentication required. Replace {{ID}} with a real id.

endpoints.name: Enable the public interface of a Load Balancer
endpoints.description: Enable the public interface of a Load Balancer. The Load Balancer will be accessible from the internet via its public IPs.
endpoints.notes: No authentication required. Replace {{ID}} with a real id.

endpoints.name: Remove Target
endpoints.description: Removes a target from a Load Balancer.
endpoints.notes: No authentication required. Replace {{ID}} with a real id.

endpoints.name: Update Service
endpoints.description:
Updates a Load Balancer Service.

#### Call specific error codes

| Code                       | Description                                             |
|----------------------------|---------------------------------------------------------|
| `source_port_already_used` | The source port you are trying to add is already in use |
endpoints.notes: No authentication required. Replace {{ID}} with a real id.

endpoints.name: Get an Action for a Load Balancer
endpoints.description: Returns a specific Action for a Load Balancer.
endpoints.notes: No authentication required. Replace {{ID}} with a real id. Replace {{ACTION_ID}} with a real action_id.

endpoints.name: Get Metrics for a LoadBalancer
endpoints.description:
You must specify the type of metric to get: `open_connections`, `connections_per_second`, `requests_per_second` or `bandwidth`. You can also specify more than one type by comma separation, e.g. `requests_per_second,bandwidth`.

Depending on the type you will get different time series data:

|Type | Timeseries | Unit | Description |
|---- |------------|------|-------------|
| open_connections | open_connections | number | Open connections |
| connections_per_second | connections_per_second | connections/s | Connections per second |
| requests_per_second | requests_per_second | requests/s | Requests per second |
| bandwidth | bandwidth.in | bytes/s | Ingress bandwidth |
|| bandwidth.out | bytes/s | Egress bandwidth |

Metrics are available for the last 30 days only.

If you do not provide the step argument we will automatically adjust it so that 200 samples are returned.

We limit the number of samples to a maximum of 500 and will adjust the step parameter accordingly.
endpoints.notes: No authentication required. Replace {{ID}} with a real id.

endpoints.name: Get all Locations
endpoints.description: Returns all Location objects.
endpoints.notes: No authentication required.

endpoints.name: Get a Location
endpoints.description: Returns a specific Location object.
endpoints.notes: No authentication required. Replace {{ID}} with a real id.

endpoints.name: Get all Networks
endpoints.description: Gets all existing networks that you have available.
endpoints.notes: No authentication required.

endpoints.name: Create a Network
endpoints.description:
Creates a network with the specified `ip_range`.

You may specify one or more `subnets`. You can also add more Subnets later by using the [add subnet action](https://docs.hetzner.cloud/#network-actions-add-a-subnet-to-a-network). If you do not specify an `ip_range` in the subnet we will automatically pick the first available /24 range for you.

You may specify one or more routes in `routes`. You can also add more routes later by using the [add route action](https://docs.hetzner.cloud/#network-actions-add-a-route-to-a-network).
endpoints.notes: No authentication required.

endpoints.name: Get a Network
endpoints.description: Gets a specific network object.
endpoints.notes: No authentication required. Replace {{ID}} with a real id.

endpoints.name: Update a Network
endpoints.description:
Updates the network properties.

Note that when updating labels, the network’s current set of labels will be replaced with the labels provided in the request body. So, for example, if you want to add a new label, you have to provide all existing labels plus the new label in the request body.

Note: if the network object changes during the request, the response will be a “conflict” error.
endpoints.notes: No authentication required. Replace {{ID}} with a real id.

endpoints.name: Delete a Network
endpoints.description:
Deletes a network. If there are Servers attached they will be detached in the background.

Note: if the network object changes during the request, the response will be a “conflict” error.
endpoints.notes: No authentication required. Replace {{ID}} with a real id.

endpoints.name: Get all Actions for a Network
endpoints.description: Returns all Action objects for a Network. You can sort the results by using the `sort` URI parameter, and filter them with the `status` parameter.
endpoints.notes: No authentication required. Replace {{ID}} with a real id.

endpoints.name: Add a route to a Network
endpoints.description:
Adds a route entry to a Network.

Note: if the Network object changes during the request, the response will be a “conflict” error.
endpoints.notes: No authentication required. Replace {{ID}} with a real id.

endpoints.name: Add a subnet to a Network
endpoints.description:
Adds a new subnet object to the Network. If you do not specify an `ip_range` for the subnet we will automatically pick the first available /24 range for you if possible.

Note: if the parent Network object changes during the request, the response will be a “conflict” error.
endpoints.notes: No authentication required. Replace {{ID}} with a real id.

endpoints.name: Change IP range of a Network
endpoints.description:
Changes the IP range of a Network. IP ranges can only be extended and never shrunk. You can only add IPs at the end of an existing IP range. This means that the IP part of your existing range must stay the same and you can only change its netmask.

For example if you have a range `10.0.0.0/16` you want to extend then your new range must also start with the IP `10.0.0.0`. Your CIDR netmask `/16` may change to a number that is smaller than `16` thereby increasing the IP range. So valid entries would be `10.0.0.0/15`, `10.0.0.0/14`, `10.0.0.0/13` and so on.

After changing the IP range you will have to adjust the routes on your connected Servers by either rebooting them or manually changing the routes to your private Network interface.

Note: if the Network object changes during the request, the response will be a “conflict” error.
endpoints.notes: No authentication required. Replace {{ID}} with a real id.

endpoints.name: Change Network Protection
endpoints.description:
Changes the protection configuration of a Network.

Note: if the Network object changes during the request, the response will be a “conflict” error.
endpoints.notes: No authentication required. Replace {{ID}} with a real id.

endpoints.name: Delete a route from a Network
endpoints.description:
Delete a route entry from a Network.

Note: if the Network object changes during the request, the response will be a “conflict” error.
endpoints.notes: No authentication required. Replace {{ID}} with a real id.

endpoints.name: Delete a subnet from a Network
endpoints.description:
Deletes a single subnet entry from a Network. You cannot delete subnets which still have Servers attached. If you have Servers attached you first need to detach all Servers that use IPs from this subnet before you can delete the subnet.

Note: if the Network object changes during the request, the response will be a “conflict” error.
endpoints.notes: No authentication required. Replace {{ID}} with a real id.

endpoints.name: Get an Action for a Network
endpoints.description: Returns a specific Action for a Network.
endpoints.notes: No authentication required. Replace {{ID}} with a real id. Replace {{ACTION_ID}} with a real action_id.

endpoints.name: Get all PlacementGroups
endpoints.description: Returns all PlacementGroup objects.
endpoints.notes: No authentication required.

endpoints.name: Create a PlacementGroup
endpoints.description: Creates a new PlacementGroup.
endpoints.notes: No authentication required.

endpoints.name: Get a PlacementGroup
endpoints.description: Gets a specific PlacementGroup object.
endpoints.notes: No authentication required. Replace {{ID}} with a real id.

endpoints.name: Update a PlacementGroup
endpoints.description:
Updates the PlacementGroup properties.

Note that when updating labels, the PlacementGroup’s current set of labels will be replaced with the labels provided in the request body. So, for example, if you want to add a new label, you have to provide all existing labels plus the new label in the request body.

Note: if the PlacementGroup object changes during the request, the response will be a “conflict” error.
endpoints.notes: No authentication required. Replace {{ID}} with a real id.

endpoints.name: Delete a PlacementGroup
endpoints.description: Deletes a PlacementGroup.
endpoints.notes: No authentication required. Replace {{ID}} with a real id.

endpoints.name: Get all prices
endpoints.description:
Returns prices for all resources available on the platform. VAT and currency of the Project owner are used for calculations.

Both net and gross prices are included in the response.
endpoints.notes: No authentication required.

endpoints.name: Get all Primary IPs
endpoints.description: Returns all Primary IP objects.
endpoints.notes: No authentication required.

endpoints.name: Create a Primary IP
endpoints.description:
Creates a new Primary IP, optionally assigned to a Server.

If you want to create a Primary IP that is not assigned to a Server, you need to provide the `datacenter` key instead of `assignee_id`. This can be either the ID or the name of the Datacenter this Primary IP shall be created in.

Note that a Primary IP can only be assigned to a Server in the same Datacenter later on.

#### Call specific error codes

| Code                          | Description                                                   |
|------------------------------ |-------------------------------------------------------------- |
| `server_not_stopped`          | The specified server is running, but needs to be powered off  |
| `server_has_ipv4`             | The server already has an ipv4 address                        |
| `server_has_ipv6`             | The server already has an ipv6 address                        |
endpoints.notes: No authentication required.

endpoints.name: Get a Primary IP
endpoints.description: Returns a specific Primary IP object.
endpoints.notes: No authentication required. Replace {{ID}} with a real id.

endpoints.name: Update a Primary IP
endpoints.description:
Updates the Primary IP.

Note that when updating labels, the Primary IP's current set of labels will be replaced with the labels provided in the request body. So, for example, if you want to add a new label, you have to provide all existing labels plus the new label in the request body.

If the Primary IP object changes during the request, the response will be a “conflict” error.
endpoints.notes: No authentication required. Replace {{ID}} with a real id.

endpoints.name: Delete a Primary IP
endpoints.description:
Deletes a Primary IP.

The Primary IP may be assigned to a Server. In this case it is unassigned automatically. The Server must be powered off (status `off`) in order for this operation to succeed.
endpoints.notes: No authentication required. Replace {{ID}} with a real id.

endpoints.name: Assign a Primary IP to a resource
endpoints.description:
Assigns a Primary IP to a Server.

A Server can only have one Primary IP of type `ipv4` and one of type `ipv6` assigned. If you need more IPs use Floating IPs.

The Server must be powered off (status `off`) in order for this operation to succeed.

#### Call specific error codes

| Code                          | Description                                                   |
|------------------------------ |-------------------------------------------------------------- |
| `server_not_stopped`          | The server is running, but needs to be powered off            |
| `primary_ip_already_assigned` | Primary ip is already assigned to a different server          |
| `server_has_ipv4`             | The server already has an ipv4 address                        |
| `server_has_ipv6`             | The server already has an ipv6 address                        |
endpoints.notes: No authentication required. Replace {{ID}} with a real id.

endpoints.name: Change reverse DNS entry for a Primary IP
endpoints.description: Changes the hostname that will appear when getting the hostname belonging to this Primary IP.
endpoints.notes: No authentication required. Replace {{ID}} with a real id.

endpoints.name: Change Primary IP Protection
endpoints.description:
Changes the protection configuration of a Primary IP.

A Primary IP can only be delete protected if its `auto_delete` property is set to `false`.
endpoints.notes: No authentication required. Replace {{ID}} with a real id.

endpoints.name: Unassign a Primary IP from a resource
endpoints.description:
Unassigns a Primary IP from a Server.

The Server must be powered off (status `off`) in order for this operation to succeed.

Note that only Servers that have at least one network interface (public or private) attached can be powered on.

#### Call specific error codes

| Code                              | Description                                                   |
|---------------------------------- |-------------------------------------------------------------- |
| `server_not_stopped`              | The server is running, but needs to be powered off            |
| `server_is_load_balancer_target`  | The server ipv4 address is a loadbalancer target              |
endpoints.notes: No authentication required. Replace {{ID}} with a real id.

endpoints.name: Get all Server Types
endpoints.description: Gets all Server type objects.
endpoints.notes: No authentication required.

endpoints.name: Get a Server Type
endpoints.description: Gets a specific Server type object.
endpoints.notes: No authentication required. Replace {{ID}} with a real id.

endpoints.name: Get all Servers
endpoints.description: Returns all existing Server objects
endpoints.notes: No authentication required.

endpoints.name: Create a Server
endpoints.description: Creates a new Server. Returns preliminary information about the Server as well as an Action that covers progress of creation.
endpoints.notes: No authentication required.

endpoints.name: Get a Server
endpoints.description: Returns a specific Server object. The Server must exist inside the Project
endpoints.notes: No authentication required. Replace {{ID}} with a real id.

endpoints.name: Update a Server
endpoints.description:
Updates a Server. You can update a Server’s name and a Server’s labels.
Please note that Server names must be unique per Project and valid hostnames as per RFC 1123 (i.e. may only contain letters, digits, periods, and dashes).
Also note that when updating labels, the Server’s current set of labels will be replaced with the labels provided in the request body. So, for example, if you want to add a new label, you have to provide all existing labels plus the new label in the request body.
endpoints.notes: No authentication required. Replace {{ID}} with a real id.

endpoints.name: Delete a Server
endpoints.description: Deletes a Server. This immediately removes the Server from your account, and it is no longer accessible.
endpoints.notes: No authentication required. Replace {{ID}} with a real id.

endpoints.name: Get all Actions for a Server
endpoints.description: Returns all Action objects for a Server. You can `sort` the results by using the sort URI parameter, and filter them with the `status` parameter.
endpoints.notes: No authentication required. Replace {{ID}} with a real id.

endpoints.name: Add a Server to a Placement Group
endpoints.description:
Adds a Server to a Placement Group.

Server must be powered off for this command to succeed.

#### Call specific error codes

| Code                          | Description                                                          |
|-------------------------------|----------------------------------------------------------------------|
| `server_not_stopped`          | The action requires a stopped server                                 |
endpoints.notes: No authentication required. Replace {{ID}} with a real id.

endpoints.name: Attach an ISO to a Server
endpoints.description:
Attaches an ISO to a Server. The Server will immediately see it as a new disk. An already attached ISO will automatically be detached before the new ISO is attached.

Servers with attached ISOs have a modified boot order: They will try to boot from the ISO first before falling back to hard disk.
endpoints.notes: No authentication required. Replace {{ID}} with a real id.

endpoints.name: Attach a Server to a Network
endpoints.description:
Attaches a Server to a network. This will complement the fixed public Server interface by adding an additional ethernet interface to the Server which is connected to the specified network.

The Server will get an IP auto assigned from a subnet of type `server` in the same `network_zone`.

Using the `alias_ips` attribute you can also define one or more additional IPs to the Servers. Please note that you will have to configure these IPs by hand on your Server since only the primary IP will be given out by DHCP.

**Call specific error codes**

| Code                             | Description                                                           |
|----------------------------------|-----------------------------------------------------------------------|
| `server_already_attached`        | The server is already attached to the network                         |
| `ip_not_available`               | The provided Network IP is not available                              |
| `no_subnet_available`            | No Subnet or IP is available for the Server within the network        |
| `networks_overlap`               | The network IP range overlaps with one of the server networks         |
endpoints.notes: No authentication required. Replace {{ID}} with a real id.

endpoints.name: Change alias IPs of a Network
endpoints.description: Changes the alias IPs of an already attached Network. Note that the existing aliases for the specified Network will be replaced with these provided in the request body. So if you want to add an alias IP, you have to provide the existing ones from the Network plus the new alias IP in the request body.
endpoints.notes: No authentication required. Replace {{ID}} with a real id.

endpoints.name: Change reverse DNS entry for this Server
endpoints.description:
Changes the hostname that will appear when getting the hostname belonging to the primary IPs (IPv4 and IPv6) of this Server.

Floating IPs assigned to the Server are not affected by this.
endpoints.notes: No authentication required. Replace {{ID}} with a real id.

endpoints.name: Change Server Protection
endpoints.description: Changes the protection configuration of the Server.
endpoints.notes: No authentication required. Replace {{ID}} with a real id.

endpoints.name: Change the Type of a Server
endpoints.description:
Changes the type (Cores, RAM and disk sizes) of a Server.

Server must be powered off for this command to succeed.

This copies the content of its disk, and starts it again.

You can only migrate to Server types with the same `storage_type` and equal or bigger disks. Shrinking disks is not possible as it might destroy data.

If the disk gets upgraded, the Server type can not be downgraded any more. If you plan to downgrade the Server type, set `upgrade_disk` to `false`.

#### Call specific error codes

| Code                          | Description                                                          |
|-------------------------------|----------------------------------------------------------------------|
| `invalid_server_type`         | The server type does not fit for the given server or is deprecated   |
| `server_not_stopped`          | The action requires a stopped server                                 |
endpoints.notes: No authentication required. Replace {{ID}} with a real id.

endpoints.name: Create Image from a Server
endpoints.description:
Creates an Image (snapshot) from a Server by copying the contents of its disks. This creates a snapshot of the current state of the disk and copies it into an Image. If the Server is currently running you must make sure that its disk content is consistent. Otherwise, the created Image may not be readable.

To make sure disk content is consistent, we recommend to shut down the Server prior to creating an Image.

You can either create a `backup` Image that is bound to the Server and therefore will be deleted when the Server is deleted, or you can create an `snapshot` Image which is completely independent of the Server it was created from and will survive Server deletion. Backup Images are only available when the backup option is enabled for the Server. Snapshot Images are billed on a per GB basis.
endpoints.notes: No authentication required. Replace {{ID}} with a real id.

endpoints.name: Detach a Server from a Network
endpoints.description: Detaches a Server from a network. The interface for this network will vanish.
endpoints.notes: No authentication required. Replace {{ID}} with a real id.

endpoints.name: Detach an ISO from a Server
endpoints.description: Detaches an ISO from a Server. In case no ISO Image is attached to the Server, the status of the returned Action is immediately set to `success`
endpoints.notes: No authentication required. Replace {{ID}} with a real id.

endpoints.name: Disable Backups for a Server
endpoints.description:
Disables the automatic backup option and deletes all existing Backups for a Server. No more additional charges for backups will be made.

Caution: This immediately removes all existing backups for the Server!
endpoints.notes: No authentication required. Replace {{ID}} with a real id.

endpoints.name: Disable Rescue Mode for a Server
endpoints.description:
Disables the Hetzner Rescue System for a Server. This makes a Server start from its disks on next reboot.

Rescue Mode is automatically disabled when you first boot into it or if you do not use it for 60 minutes.

Disabling rescue mode will not reboot your Server — you will have to do this yourself.
endpoints.notes: No authentication required. Replace {{ID}} with a real id.

endpoints.name: Enable and Configure Backups for a Server
endpoints.description:
Enables and configures the automatic daily backup option for the Server. Enabling automatic backups will increase the price of the Server by 20%. In return, you will get seven slots where Images of type backup can be stored.

Backups are automatically created daily.
endpoints.notes: No authentication required. Replace {{ID}} with a real id.

endpoints.name: Enable Rescue Mode for a Server
endpoints.description:
Enable the Hetzner Rescue System for this Server. The next time a Server with enabled rescue mode boots it will start a special minimal Linux distribution designed for repair and reinstall.

In case a Server cannot boot on its own you can use this to access a Server’s disks.

Rescue Mode is automatically disabled when you first boot into it or if you do not use it for 60 minutes.

Enabling rescue mode will not [reboot](https://docs.hetzner.cloud/#server-actions-soft-reboot-a-server) your Server — you will have to do this yourself.
endpoints.notes: No authentication required. Replace {{ID}} with a real id.

endpoints.name: Power off a Server
endpoints.description: Cuts power to the Server. This forcefully stops it without giving the Server operating system time to gracefully stop. May lead to data loss, equivalent to pulling the power cord. Power off should only be used when shutdown does not work.
endpoints.notes: No authentication required. Replace {{ID}} with a real id.

endpoints.name: Power on a Server
endpoints.description: Starts a Server by turning its power on.
endpoints.notes: No authentication required. Replace {{ID}} with a real id.

endpoints.name: Soft-reboot a Server
endpoints.description: Reboots a Server gracefully by sending an ACPI request. The Server operating system must support ACPI and react to the request, otherwise the Server will not reboot.
endpoints.notes: No authentication required. Replace {{ID}} with a real id.

endpoints.name: Rebuild a Server from an Image
endpoints.description:
Rebuilds a Server overwriting its disk with the content of an Image, thereby **destroying all data** on the target Server

The Image can either be one you have created earlier (`backup` or `snapshot` Image) or it can be a completely fresh `system` Image provided by us. You can get a list of all available Images with `GET /images`.

Your Server will automatically be powered off before the rebuild command executes.
endpoints.notes: No authentication required. Replace {{ID}} with a real id.

endpoints.name: Remove from Placement Group
endpoints.description: Removes a Server from a Placement Group.
endpoints.notes: No authentication required. Replace {{ID}} with a real id.

endpoints.name: Reset a Server
endpoints.description: Cuts power to a Server and starts it again. This forcefully stops it without giving the Server operating system time to gracefully stop. This may lead to data loss, it’s equivalent to pulling the power cord and plugging it in again. Reset should only be used when reboot does not work.
endpoints.notes: No authentication required. Replace {{ID}} with a real id.

endpoints.name: Reset root Password of a Server
endpoints.description:
Resets the root password. Only works for Linux systems that are running the qemu guest agent. Server must be powered on (status `running`) in order for this operation to succeed.

This will generate a new password for this Server and return it.

If this does not succeed you can use the rescue system to netboot the Server and manually change your Server password by hand.
endpoints.notes: No authentication required. Replace {{ID}} with a real id.

endpoints.name: Shutdown a Server
endpoints.description: Shuts down a Server gracefully by sending an ACPI shutdown request. The Server operating system must support ACPI and react to the request, otherwise the Server will not shut down.
endpoints.notes: No authentication required. Replace {{ID}} with a real id.

endpoints.name: Get an Action for a Server
endpoints.description: Returns a specific Action object for a Server.
endpoints.notes: No authentication required. Replace {{ID}} with a real id. Replace {{ACTION_ID}} with a real action_id.

endpoints.name: Get Metrics for a Server
endpoints.description:
Get Metrics for specified Server.

You must specify the type of metric to get: cpu, disk or network. You can also specify more than one type by comma separation, e.g. cpu,disk.

Depending on the type you will get different time series data

| Type    | Timeseries              | Unit      | Description                                          |
|---------|-------------------------|-----------|------------------------------------------------------|
| cpu     | cpu                     | percent   | Percent CPU usage                                    |
| disk    | disk.0.iops.read        | iop/s     | Number of read IO operations per second              |
|         | disk.0.iops.write       | iop/s     | Number of write IO operations per second             |
|         | disk.0.bandwidth.read   | bytes/s   | Bytes read per second                                |
|         | disk.0.bandwidth.write  | bytes/s   | Bytes written per second                             |
| network | network.0.pps.in        | packets/s | Public Network interface packets per second received |
|         | network.0.pps.out       | packets/s | Public Network interface packets per second sent     |
|         | network.0.bandwidth.in  | bytes/s   | Public Network interface bytes/s received            |
|         | network.0.bandwidth.out | bytes/s   | Public Network interface bytes/s sent                |

Metrics are available for the last 30 days only.

If you do not provide the step argument we will automatically adjust it so that a maximum of 200 samples are returned.

We limit the number of samples returned to a maximum of 500 and will adjust the step parameter accordingly.
endpoints.notes: No authentication required. Replace {{ID}} with a real id.

endpoints.name: Get all SSH keys
endpoints.description: Returns all SSH key objects.
endpoints.notes: No authentication required.

endpoints.name: Create an SSH key
endpoints.description: Creates a new SSH key with the given `name` and `public_key`. Once an SSH key is created, it can be used in other calls such as creating Servers.
endpoints.notes: No authentication required.

endpoints.name: Get a SSH key
endpoints.description: Returns a specific SSH key object.
endpoints.notes: No authentication required. Replace {{ID}} with a real id.

endpoints.name: Update an SSH key
endpoints.description:
Updates an SSH key. You can update an SSH key name and an SSH key labels.

Please note that when updating labels, the SSH key current set of labels will be replaced with the labels provided in the request body. So, for example, if you want to add a new label, you have to provide all existing labels plus the new label in the request body.
endpoints.notes: No authentication required. Replace {{ID}} with a real id.

endpoints.name: Delete an SSH key
endpoints.description: Deletes an SSH key. It cannot be used anymore.
endpoints.notes: No authentication required. Replace {{ID}} with a real id.

endpoints.name: Get all Volumes
endpoints.description: Gets all existing Volumes that you have available.
endpoints.notes: No authentication required.

endpoints.name: Create a Volume
endpoints.description:
Creates a new Volume attached to a Server. If you want to create a Volume that is not attached to a Server, you need to provide the `location` key instead of `server`. This can be either the ID or the name of the Location this Volume will be created in. Note that a Volume can be attached to a Server only in the same Location as the Volume itself.

Specifying the Server during Volume creation will automatically attach the Volume to that Server after it has been initialized. In that case, the `next_actions` key in the response is an array which contains a single `attach_volume` action.

The minimum Volume size is 10GB and the maximum size is 10TB (10240GB).

A volume’s name can consist of alphanumeric characters, dashes, underscores, and dots, but has to start and end with an alphanumeric character. The total length is limited to 64 characters. Volume names must be unique per Project.

#### Call specific error codes

| Code                                | Description                                         |
|-------------------------------------|-----------------------------------------------------|
| `no_space_left_in_location`         | There is no volume space left in the given location |
endpoints.notes: No authentication required.

endpoints.name: Get a Volume
endpoints.description: Gets a specific Volume object.
endpoints.notes: No authentication required. Replace {{ID}} with a real id.

endpoints.name: Update a Volume
endpoints.description:
Updates the Volume properties.

Note that when updating labels, the volume’s current set of labels will be replaced with the labels provided in the request body. So, for example, if you want to add a new label, you have to provide all existing labels plus the new label in the request body.
endpoints.notes: No authentication required. Replace {{ID}} with a real id.

endpoints.name: Delete a Volume
endpoints.description: Deletes a volume. All Volume data is irreversibly destroyed. The Volume must not be attached to a Server and it must not have delete protection enabled.
endpoints.notes: No authentication required. Replace {{ID}} with a real id.

endpoints.name: Get all Actions for a Volume
endpoints.description: Returns all Action objects for a Volume. You can `sort` the results by using the sort URI parameter, and filter them with the `status` parameter.
endpoints.notes: No authentication required. Replace {{ID}} with a real id.

endpoints.name: Attach Volume to a Server
endpoints.description: Attaches a Volume to a Server. Works only if the Server is in the same Location as the Volume.
endpoints.notes: No authentication required. Replace {{ID}} with a real id.

endpoints.name: Change Volume Protection
endpoints.description: Changes the protection configuration of a Volume.
endpoints.notes: No authentication required. Replace {{ID}} with a real id.

endpoints.name: Detach Volume
endpoints.description: Detaches a Volume from the Server it’s attached to. You may attach it to a Server again at a later time.
endpoints.notes: No authentication required. Replace {{ID}} with a real id.

endpoints.name: Resize Volume
endpoints.description: Changes the size of a Volume. Note that downsizing a Volume is not possible.
endpoints.notes: No authentication required. Replace {{ID}} with a real id.

endpoints.name: Get an Action for a Volume
endpoints.description: Returns a specific Action for a Volume.
endpoints.notes: No authentication required. Replace {{ID}} with a real id. Replace {{ACTION_ID}} with a real action_id.
